model_justification: All direct competitors (DairyCraftPro, Craftybase, Strinos, Wherefour) use subscription models for recurring inventory/traceability SaaS. Subscription aligns with ongoing value delivery (daily inventory tracking, batch traceability, compliance reporting). The target micro-producer segment needs predictable low monthly costs rather than large upfront purchases.

pricing_suggestion.justification: Pain intensity is medium (from Problem dept), pricing at low-to-mid competitive range. Competitive low=$24 (Craftybase), mid=$135 (Strinos). $35/mo positions just above cheapest competitors while remaining accessible to Patagonian micro-producers with limited budgets. The identified market gap is specifically for affordable tools under $50/mo.

unit_economics.churn_source: SMB SaaS benchmark 3-5% monthly (Optifai 2026 study of 939 B2B SaaS companies; MRRSaver 2026 benchmarks). Used 5% (high end) given micro-producer segment with low switching costs and seasonal cash flow variability.
unit_economics.margin_source: SaaS gross margin benchmarks 2025 (CloudZero, High Alpha). Vertical SaaS with some customization typically 60-75%; pure SaaS 75-85%. Used 75% for simple cloud SaaS with minimal services.
unit_economics.cac_source: Agri-tech SaaS CAC benchmark $400-500 (FinancialModelsLab 2026); SMB SaaS $200-700 (GenesysGrowth, FirstPageSage 2025). Used $300 assuming content marketing/community-driven acquisition in niche market, below agri-tech average due to organic channels but adjusted for geographic isolation of Patagonia.

cac_plus_20.assessment: CAC increase to $360 pushes LTV/CAC well below 2.0x and payback beyond 12 months — acquisition efficiency becomes unsustainable for micro-SaaS economics.

churn_plus_20.assessment: Churn increase to 6% monthly reduces LTV to $437.50 and LTV/CAC to 1.46x — customer lifetime shortens to 16.7 months, making unit economics unviable.

price_minus_20.assessment: Price reduction to $28/mo drops LTV to $420 and LTV/CAC to 1.40x with 14.3-month payback — the already thin margins collapse under any pricing pressure.

model_precedents.segment: Small cheese makers and dairy producers
model_precedents.evidence: Active SaaS with free tier + paid plans at $25-69/mo; specifically built for cheese production with batch tracking and HACCP compliance
142+ reviews, 95% satisfaction, $24+/mo; bootstrapped to estimated $500K-$2M ARR serving artisan food producers
Series C funded ($20M+), operates across 52+ countries, SaaS platform for farm management and traceability

model_precedents.segment: Farm management for producers across 25 countries
model_precedents.evidence: Deployed across 600,000+ acres, served 1.3M farmers; SaaS-based agriculture management platform

model_precedents.segment: Agricultural supply chain traceability for smallholder networks
model_precedents.evidence: Operates in 37 countries digitizing farm-level data for traceability and sustainability compliance

assumptions: ARPU of $35/mo based on competitive positioning between Craftybase ($24/mo) and Strinos ($95/mo), targeting the identified gap for micro-producers under $50/mo
Monthly churn rate of 5% based on high-end SMB SaaS benchmarks (Optifai 2026), reflecting micro-producer segment with seasonal cash flow and low switching costs
Gross margin of 75% based on SaaS industry benchmarks (CloudZero 2025); pure cloud SaaS with minimal professional services

CAC of $300 based on agri-tech benchmark ($400-500 from FinancialModelsLab) adjusted downward for content/community-driven acquisition in a niche market, but may be optimistic given Patagonia's geographic isolation
Payback formula: CAC / (ARPU × Gross Margin) = $300 / $26.25 = 11.43 months
No freemium conversion adjustment applied — assumed all users are paying subscribers at $35/mo
